old_claim.note: Advanced Distillation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because distillation_advanced is now honestly scoped to ninth-century medieval alembic apparatus and procedures, which cannot enable a basic_chemistry node whose unchanged first-known-date anchor is c. 270.

source_shape.source_locator: pp. 79-81 and 87-90, especially section III.2: dates al-Kindi to the ninth century (d. ca. 866) and documents his alembic, receiver, heated bath or stove, and repeated distillation procedures; the article treats earlier Greco-Egyptian practice as historical background rather than this medieval implementation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The removed prerequisite is a ninth-century medieval laboratory milestone, centuries later than the dependent node's unchanged c. 270 date.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The former generic chemistry overview did not establish this edge, and a later ninth-century milestone cannot causally enable an earlier c. 270 node. Retyping would preserve the same reverse chronology, while replacement would require evidence for a distinct earlier prerequisite outside this exact edge audit.

ontology_before: A c. 270 chemistry node depended directly on a separately scoped ninth-century medieval alembic milestone, creating reverse causation.
ontology_after: The two historical boundaries remain distinct: basic_chemistry keeps its existing c. 270 claim and other prerequisites, while medieval alembic distillation remains an 800-century milestone without a backward edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-medieval-alembic-distillation-scope: basic_chemistry must not directly depend on distillation_advanced.
would_reject_if: basic_chemistry is honestly redated to the ninth century or later after a full node-scope audit.
distillation_advanced is respecified and evidenced as a pre-270 milestone rather than medieval alembic distillation.
A distinct pre-270 distillation node is created with direct evidence and proposed as a replacement prerequisite in a separate audited change.
short_reason: The medieval alembic milestone postdates the c. 270 dependent by more than five centuries, so the direct edge is reverse-causal.
